assumptions: valid_out is asserted for exactly one cycle when four valid inputs have been accumulated.

checks.description: After four consecutive cycles with valid_in=1, data_out should reflect the sum of the four data_in values and valid_out should assert for one cycle.
notes: Timing of the assertion is not fixed; the check observes the first occurrence after the fourth valid input.
checks.pass_condition: Within a conservative observation window (e.g., up to two cycles after the fourth valid input), valid_out becomes 1 and data_out equals the arithmetic sum of the four preceding data_in values.
checks.trigger_condition: When a sequence of four cycles with valid_in asserted is detected.

checks.description: valid_out must be deasserted in all cycles except the one where the accumulation result is presented.
checks.pass_condition: valid_out is 0 in every cycle except the single cycle following the accumulation of four valid inputs.

checks.description: When valid_in is low, changes to data_in must not affect data_out or cause valid_out to assert.
notes: Covers negative behavior without assuming specific error signaling.
checks.pass_condition: During any cycle where valid_in=0, data_out remains unchanged and valid_out stays 0.

oracle_notes: All checks avoid exact-cycle assumptions due to unknown latency model.
Signal policies are inherited from baseline; no new signals introduced.
